techniques.name: Alternate Picking
techniques.description: Strict down-up-down-up pick strokes on every note. The foundation of speed and precision in metal.

techniques.name: Economy Picking
techniques.description: Combine alternate picking with sweep motion when crossing strings — fewer wasted pick strokes, more efficiency at high speed.

techniques.name: Legato
techniques.description: Smooth connected lines using hammer-ons and pull-offs with minimal picking. Focus on left-hand strength and consistency.

techniques.name: Hammer-ons
techniques.description: Fret a note without picking by hammering a finger onto the fret. Essential for legato and speed runs.

techniques.name: Pull-offs
techniques.description: Sound a lower note by pulling a fretting finger off the string. Paired with hammer-ons to form legato phrases.

techniques.name: Bends
techniques.description: Push or pull a string across the fretboard to raise pitch. Requires string control and ear training for intonation.

techniques.name: Vibrato
techniques.description: Oscillate pitch repeatedly on a sustained note. A defining expressive tool — speed, width, and consistency all matter.

techniques.name: Slides
techniques.description: Glide a fretting finger along the string to a target fret while maintaining pressure. Smooth transitions between positions.

techniques.name: Sweep Arpeggios
techniques.description: Rake the pick across strings in one fluid motion while the fretting hand plays one note per string. Pinnacle technique for arpeggiated lines at speed.

techniques.name: Tapping
techniques.description: Use a picking-hand finger to hammer onto the fretboard, extending legato reach across the neck. Classic metal showpiece.

techniques.name: String Skipping
techniques.description: Skip over one or more strings between notes, creating wide intervallic leaps. Demands pick accuracy and muting discipline.

techniques.name: Hybrid Picking
techniques.description: Combine pick and fingers simultaneously, allowing simultaneous independent voices or wide interval jumps without sweep motion.

techniques.name: Pinch Harmonics
techniques.description: Touch the string with the pick-hand thumb immediately after striking to produce a harmonic squeal. Embellishment central to metal tone.

techniques.name: Palm Muting
techniques.description: Rest the pick-hand palm lightly on the strings near the bridge to dampen sustain. Creates the chunky, chugging sound core to metal rhythm.

techniques.name: Gallop Picking
techniques.description: A triplet-feel rhythm pattern (long-short-short or short-short-long) defining classic thrash and power metal. Heavy emphasis on right-hand consistency.

techniques.name: Tremolo Picking
techniques.description: Sustain a single note by picking it as fast as possible with constant alternate strokes. Core to black metal and thrash lead lines.
